response:
To compute the **sample variance** of the data set:

$$
\{-22, 22, 11, -19, 8, 7\}
$$

---

### Step 1: Find the mean (average) of the data set

$$
\text{Mean} = \frac{-22 + 22 + 11 - 19 + 8 + 7}{6}
$$

$$
\text{Mean} = \frac{1}{6} \times ( -22 + 22 + 11 - 19 + 8 + 7 ) = \frac{1}{6} \times 1 = \frac{1}{6}
$$

---

### Step 2: Subtract the mean from each data point and square the result

$$
\begin{align*}
(-22 - \frac{1}{6})^2 &= \left(-\frac{133}{6}\right)^2 = \frac{17689}{36} \\
22 - \frac{1}{6} &= \frac{133}{6} \Rightarrow \left(\frac{133}{6}\right)^2 = \frac{17689}{36} \\
11 - \frac{1}{6} &= \frac{65}{6} \Rightarrow \left(\frac{65}{6}\right)^2 = \frac{4225}{36} \\
-19 - \frac{1}{6} &= -\frac{113}{6} \Rightarrow \left(-\frac{113}{6}\right)^2 = \frac{12769}{36} \\
8 - \frac{1}{6} &= \frac{47}{6} \Rightarrow \left(\frac{47}{6}\right)^2 = \frac{2209}{36} \\
7 - \frac{1}{6} &= \frac{41}{6} \Rightarrow \left(\frac{41}{6}\right)^2 = \frac{1681}{36} \\
\end{align*}
$$
